source: Circuit Breaker Patterns for AI Agent Pipelines (Cordum)
summary: Three-state circuit breaker (Closed/Open/Half-Open) adapted specifically for AI agent subprocess failures, with 5-failure/60-second production consensus and model-downgrade fallback chains.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses AIBlocked dead-end state; model downgrade chain maps to CLAUDE_FALLBACK_MODELS env var

summary: Self-healing router using Dijkstra shortest-path on a tool graph; reweights failed tools to infinity and reroutes without invoking the LLM, reserving LLM for unresolvable cases.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — formal analogue of routing around dead subprocess; co-inspiration for HID-108

source: AgentFactory: Self-Evolving Framework via Executable Subagent Accumulation (arxiv 2603.18000)
summary: Stores successful task solutions as executable Python subagents indexed by task type; retrieves and reuses them, reducing orchestration cost by 57% vs ReAct baseline.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly maps to AgentForge's no-cross-run-memory limitation; distilled plan summaries indexed by Linear labels

source: Scaling Test-Time Compute for Agentic Coding (arxiv 2604.16529)
summary: Converts agent rollouts into compact structured summaries capturing hypotheses, progress, and failure modes; uses RTV for parallel selection and PDR for sequential refinement. Improved Claude 4.5-Opus from 70.9% to 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — PDR pattern directly maps to rollout summaries seeding retry attempts; inspired HID-110's structured rollout summary artifact

summary: Shows that scaling test-time compute for software engineering agents (extending reasoning rather than using larger models) yields significant performance gains; analyzes sequential vs parallel scaling strategies.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — co-inspiration for HID-110 complexity-tiered timeouts

summary: Eliminates meta-agent/target-agent distinction; agent edits its own Python source to improve benchmark performance from 17% to 53% on SWE-Bench Verified subset.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — self-editing own code is too risky for production orchestrator; reflection mechanism inspires plan-repair concepts

summary: Evolutionary archive of coding agent variants; agents rewrite their own Python codebase, empirically validate each variant, and archive successful changes. Improved SWE-bench from 20% to 50%.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — evolutionary archive concept interesting but requires significant rewrite of AgentForge itself; too architectural for 1-2 sprint scope

summary: First agent to perform live runtime self-evolution — creates and revises custom tools on-the-fly during active problem-solving via lightweight step-reflection prompt. 45.8% on SWE-Bench Pro.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — step-reflection is interesting but in-session tool creation is hard to implement in CLI subprocess model without architectural changes

summary: Self-improving open-source agent (95k GitHub stars) powered by GEPA which reads full execution traces, proposes targeted mutations to skill documents and prompts, distills reusable Skill Documents every ~15 tasks. 40% faster on tasks with 20+ skills.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — execution-trace-to-skill distillation directly applicable; but self-modification of agent prompts requires human approval workflow

summary: Historical Lesson Learning Mechanism distills insights from prior failed repair attempts and injects them into subsequent correction passes, preventing repetitive failure cycles. Accepted ICSE 2026.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — HLLM is essentially what HID-110 implements as rollout summaries; paper provides rigorous theoretical backing

source: CausalFlow: Causal Attribution and Counterfactual Repair (arxiv 2605.25338)
summary: Converts failed execution traces into minimal counterfactual repairs using Causal Responsibility Score (CRS) to identify exactly which step caused failure. Most LLM failures are localized to 1-2 steps.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — step-level failure attribution is more sophisticated than HID-110's rollout summaries; candidate for future enhancement

summary: Systematic study showing task performance and budget-awareness are uncorrelated (r=0.35); frontier agents waste 28-64% of tokens on tasks they will fail. SFT+RL training saves 64% on failed trajectories.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — quantifies the waste from uniform timeout policy; validates HID-110 complexity-tiered approach

summary: Lightweight router predicts minimum reasoning effort per step; achieves 52.7% token reduction on TAU-Bench Retail with maintained accuracy. Requires integration with model reasoning controls.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — per-step routing requires model-level effort API; not directly applicable to CLI subprocess model

summary: Budget-conditioned node selection uses remaining resource ratio as scaling exponent; 5-call BAVT consistently surpasses 20-call baseline. Residual value predictor prunes uninformative tool calls.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — tree search architecture requires non-trivial infrastructure changes; valuable as future direction

source: MemRL: Self-Evolving Agents via RL on Episodic Memory (arxiv 2601.03192)
summary: Updates Q-values on stored episodic memories via environmental feedback; Two-Phase Retrieval filters by semantic relevance then selects by learned utility score. Outperforms SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — addresses no-memory-across-runs limitation; more sophisticated than label-based retrieval in HID-109; candidate for future V2 of skill memory

source: CTIM-Rover: Pitfalls of Episodic Memory in SE Agents (arxiv 2505.23422)
summary: Critical warning: adding naive embedding-based episodic memory to AutoCodeRover hurt performance (40% vs 42% baseline) due to retrieval noise injecting distracting episodes. Exact label matching in HID-109 avoids this failure mode.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — validates HID-109's decision to use exact label matching (not semantic embedding) for MVP skill memory retrieval

summary: Induces abstract reusable workflows from successful trajectories; online mode extracts workflows from agent's own test-time experience with zero labeled examples. Tested on Mind2Web and WebArena.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — direct inspiration for HID-109; AgentFactory's approach was more practically framed for the initial implementation

summary: Passes partial outputs from upstream agents to downstream agents before upstream finishes; achieves up to 93% TTFT reduction in sequential pipelines. Requires streaming-capable agent subprocess.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — interesting but requires major streaming infrastructure changes to ProcessRunner; CLI agents currently buffer all output until completion

source: Speculative Actions: Lossless Framework for Faster AI Agents (arxiv 2510.04371)
summary: ICLR 2026 Oral. Predicts future agent actions using draft model while current action's API call processes; commits correct predictions, rolls back mispredictions. Up to 55% prediction accuracy, ~30% latency reduction.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — action reversibility requirement makes this hard to implement for file-writing CLI subprocess agents; monitor for rollback mechanism maturity

source: M1-Parallel: Sequential Multi-Step Tasks with Parallel LLM Agents (arxiv 2507.08944)
summary: Runs multiple complete multi-agent teams concurrently; early termination achieves 1.6-2.2x speedup at 2.5-3.4x cost increase. Solves the parallel-branch-and-pick-winner problem.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — strong concept but requires parallel agent execution infrastructure; AgentForge currently sequential; evaluate after HID-108/109/110 are done

summary: Decomposes tasks into DAG of subtasks; parallel scheduler executes dependency-free nodes simultaneously. Open source code available. Requires LLM to produce valid DAGs.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — DAG planning is powerful but requires architectural change to sequential state machine; future direction after parallel execution infrastructure exists

summary: Merges AutoGen + Semantic Kernel; ConcurrentBuilder for parallel fan-out, add_multi_selection_edge_group for dynamic routing, built-in checkpoint/resume. GA April 2, 2026.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — .NET and Python only; AgentForge is TypeScript/Node.js; architectural patterns are instructive but framework is not adoptable

summary: LangGraph 1.0 GA October 2025; durable execution, streaming, human-in-the-loop, dual memory (short+long term). TypeScript SDK at full parity. Postgres checkpointer for crash recovery.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge has its own state machine; LangGraph's Postgres checkpointer is a reference implementation for crash recovery patterns in HID-108

source: DBOS: Durable Execution for Crashproof AI Agents (dbos.dev)
summary: Automatically checkpoints agent progress into Postgres; resumes from last checkpointed step on crash. Exactly-once guarantee within same Postgres instance.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — Temporal/DBOS are full workflow engines; HID-108's circuit breaker is a lighter-weight approach that fits AgentForge's architecture

summary: Maps observable failure signals to inferred failure classes (transient/permanent/semantic) and selects targeted recovery actions under explicit budgets. June 2026.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — failure taxonomy is the right mental model for HID-108; the paper validates the design of different recovery actions per failure type

summary: Production-battle-tested memory layer; hybrid vector+graph retrieval; 91% lower p95 latency vs full-context; 90% token reduction. JavaScript SDK available.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — production-ready memory layer that could back HID-109's AiSkillMemory; AgentFactory's exact-label approach chosen for MVP to avoid CTIM-Rover retrieval noise

summary: Modular classification of failure modes across memory/reflection/planning/action/system levels; AgentDebug achieves 26% relative task-success improvement by targeting root-cause failures.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — taxonomy provides vocabulary for classifying AIBlocked failure types; useful for designing HID-108 failure classification logic

summary: Source-code analysis of top-performing coding agent scaffolds; error recovery mechanisms and adaptive context compaction are most impactful components, more so than the underlying model.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly validates prioritizing orchestration improvements (HID-108/109/110) over model upgrades; error recovery ranks #1

source: VIGIL: Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing LLM Agents (arxiv 2512.07094)
summary: Sidecar supervisor that scores events into an EmoBank, diagnoses failures via Roses/Buds/Thorns, and outputs targeted prompt+code patches to unstick agents. December 2025.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — sidecar supervisor pattern is interesting for HID-108's fallback logic but the approach is complex; monitor for production adoption

summary: U-shaped entropy pattern across LLMs; lightweight entropy-based probe classifies inputs into Easy/Normal/Hard tiers for routing. 22.4% token reduction, no accuracy loss.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — entropy-based difficulty classifier is a lightweight addition to HID-110's ComplexityEstimator; candidate for upgrading the complexity estimation logic

summary: CLI coding agent reaching #1 on Terminal-Bench at 42.5% using git-backed memory, skills libraries, and continual learning. Outperforms larger models due to memory substituting for raw scale.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — validates HID-109's premise: memory beats model scale for repeat patterns; Node.js 18+ compatible

summary: NVIDIA Research. Trains small orchestrator model via end-to-end RL with outcome/efficiency/preference rewards to learn when to retry, escalate, or stop calling tools.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — trainable routing layer is compelling but requires RL training infrastructure; consider after AgentForge has sufficient execution trace logs

summary: Orchestrated test-time compute for long-horizon agentic tasks; demonstrates that hierarchical orchestration with selective compute allocation improves SWE-bench performance.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — validates the complexity-tiered approach in HID-110; paper-specific techniques not directly adoptable
